decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective immediate action is to increase the thermal conductivity of the heat spreader to enhance lateral heat dissipation away from the chip hot spot.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity by 1.5x improves lateral heat spreading, reducing the thermal gradient and lowering the peak chip temperature.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if conductivity becomes too high relative to the base plate extraction capacity
Material cost or availability constraints not modeled here might limit real-world feasibility of very high conductivity materials
